name: Negeri Sembilan (Malaysia)

colors.symbolism: the Yang di-Pertuan Besar (the elected ruler)

description: A field divided per fess (horizontally) into three unequal stripes of yellow over black over red.
history: The state flag of Negeri Sembilan ('Nine States'), in central Peninsular Malaysia. The yellow band represents the Yang di-Pertuan Besar (the elective ruler), black represents the territorial chiefs (Undang), and red represents the people.

features.symbolism: the three pillars of the Negeri Sembilan adat: the elected ruler, the territorial chiefs, and the people
